assumptions: The developer has a supported local machine and can install CLI tools.
No Terraform binary, provider credentials, working directory, or managed resource exists yet.

developer_goal: Install Terraform, choose a Get Started provider route, and reach that route's first applied infrastructure success.

first_success.outcome: For the representative Get Started - Docker candidate, terraform apply creates the tutorial resources and localhost:8000 serves nginx.
first_success.observable_signal: terraform apply reports resources added and localhost:8000 serves the started nginx container.

route_selection.surface: HashiCorp Terraform Get Started provider tutorials
route_selection.rule: Do not invent a winner among peer Get Started providers; select one only when the corpus names a provider and its account or credential boundary.

candidates.name: Get Started - Docker local nginx container
candidates.reason: Documented end-to-end CLI path with a local success signal, but Terraform's homepage lists it as one peer beside AWS, Azure, GCP, Oracle Cloud, and HCP Terraform.

candidates.name: Get Started - AWS, Azure, GCP, or Oracle Cloud
candidates.reason: Peer cloud tutorials require provider accounts and credentials and produce different first resources.

candidates.name: HCP Terraform hosted workflow
candidates.reason: Homepage presents HCP Terraform as a distinct get-started and collaboration surface with its own account boundary.

required_path.action: Create any provider or HCP account required by the selected Get Started route.

required_fields.label: Provider or HCP account fields
required_fields.notes: Docker-local route may need no cloud account; AWS and HCP routes require external account creation whose fields are not fixed until the provider is chosen.

required_path.observable_result: Credentials or local runtime prerequisites for the selected provider route are available.

required_path.action: Install the Terraform CLI for the selected operating system.
required_path.observable_result: terraform -help or terraform version runs successfully.

required_path.action: For the Docker candidate, install Docker, write main.tf, initialize, apply, and verify nginx.

required_fields.label: terraform apply approval
required_fields.notes: Apply requires typing yes to approve the plan in the CLI tutorials.

required_path.observable_result: Apply completes and localhost:8000 serves the nginx container.

prerequisites.description: For the Docker candidate, a working local Docker Engine or Desktop CLI is required before apply.

external_gates.description: Cloud Get Started routes require authenticated access to the chosen cloud provider account.

unavoidable_waits.description: terraform init must download providers, and apply must pull images or provision remote resources before verification.

platform_outcomes.description: Terraform creates the declared Docker image and container resources and reports apply results.

excluded.item: Later Docker tutorial chapters on change, destroy, variables, and outputs
excluded.reason: They occur after the first successful apply and nginx verification.

excluded.item: Reading Terraform conceptual overview pages without applying
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is not a counted developer action.

sources.title: Get Started - Docker
evidence_supported: Docker tutorial collection and first-build positioning

sources.title: Install Terraform
evidence_supported: CLI install routes and Docker prerequisite mention

sources.title: Get Started - AWS create
evidence_supported: AWS as a peer Get Started provider path

uncertainties.question: Which Terraform Get Started provider or HCP route should represent first success?
uncertainties.impact: Provider choice changes account creation, credentials, required actions, waits, and the observable resource.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: An explicit corpus provider selection, then a fresh walkthrough of that tutorial from account or local runtime prerequisites through first apply verification.

uncertainties.question: If Docker is selected, what is the complete Docker install journey counted before terraform apply?
uncertainties.impact: Docker Desktop versus Engine install burden can dominate the measured path and collide with the separate Docker platform audit.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A rule for whether Docker install steps are prerequisites or in-path actions for the Terraform Docker tutorial.
